channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: At least four killed and dozens injured in Indonesia train crash
item.description: A commuter train and a long-distance train collided on the outskirts of Jakarta on Monday, killing at least four people and injuring dozens. Officials are likely to investigate the cause of the crash.

item.title: Iran Offers Plan to Focus on Strait of Hormuz and Delay Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iran proposed opening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping traffic and lifting the U. S. blockade in a new offer delivered on Sunday. The plan delays nuclear talks with the U. S. until later.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Offers To Open Strait Of Hormuz, Gives US Conditions
item.description: Iran's minister arrived in Russia as ceasefire talks between the US, Israel, and Iran collapsed. Iran blamed the US for the collapse of the talks. US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Blames US For Collapse Of Talks As Minister Arrives In Russia NDTV.

item.title: Sam Altman's sister loses lawyers in her sexual abuse lawsuit against OpenAI CEO
item.description: Sam Altman's sister lost her lawyers in her sexual abuse lawsuit against OpenAI CEO. The case against Altman continues without her current legal representation. Sam Altman's sister loses lawyers in her sexual abuse lawsuit against OpenAI CEO.

item.title: US press gala shooting suspect charged with attempting to kill Trump
item.description: Cole Thomas Allen was charged with attempting to kill former U. S. President Trump in a shooting at a press gala on Saturday. The suspect's motives are being investigated. Prosecutor says Cole Thomas Allen 'attempted to assassinate the president of the United States' in Saturday attack.

item.title: Iran’s Foreign Minister Is in Russia for Talks With Putin on Middle East War
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Moscow to discuss the Middle East conflict. Russia remains a key player in the region. Abbas Araghchi, Iran’s foreign minister, met with President Vladimir V.

item.title: Canada Setting Up Sovereign Wealth Fund
item.description: Canada is setting up a sovereign wealth fund, which will be smaller than those in other oil-producing nations like Norway and the Middle East. The fund's size has not been disclosed.

item.title: Mexico warns US involvement in anti-drug operation should not to be repeated
item.description: Mexico's President Claudia Sheinbaum warned the US against unauthorized involvement in anti-drug operations. A diplomatic note was sent after a US presence was discovered in a Chihuahua operation that resulted in four official deaths.

item.title: U.K. PM Starmer faces possible Parliament probe over Mandelson
item.description: UK Prime Minister Starmer faces a possible Parliament probe over his involvement with Peter Mandelson. This development is part of a scandal that has led to calls for Starmer's resignation.

item.title: China vows countermeasures if EU enacts 'Made in Europe' plan
item.description: China has vowed to take countermeasures if the EU enacts its 'Made in Europe' plan. The plan requires companies to meet EU-made part thresholds to access public funds in strategic sectors.

item.title: UK-based AI startup Ineffable raises $1.1 billion in Europe's largest seed financing
item.description: UK-based AI startup Ineffable raised $1.1 billion in Europe's largest seed financing. The company did not disclose details on how the funds will be used. UK-based AI startup Ineffable raises $1.1 billion in Europe's largest seed financing.

item.title: Melania Trump urges ABC to 'take stand' on Jimmy Kimmel after widow joke
item.description: Melania Trump urged ABC to take a stand against Jimmy Kimmel after he made a joke about her as an "expectant widow" in a parody aired before the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The joke sparked criticism from the First Lady's office.

item.title: Ceasefire at risk as Pakistan and Afghanistan report cross-border attacks
item.description: Pakistan and Afghanistan reported cross-border attacks, putting a ceasefire at risk. The strikes were the first since the two countries agreed to halt violence at peace talks last month. The strikes were the first since the two agreed to halt the violence at peace talks last month.

item.title: German tourist dies after being bitten at snake show on family holiday in Egypt
item.description: A 57-year-old German tourist died after a snake bit him during a show in Egypt. The man was watching a snake-charming performance at a hotel in Hurghada when the reptile crawled into his trousers.

item.title: At least four killed in train collision on outskirts of Jakarta
item.description: At least four people were killed in a train collision on the outskirts of Jakarta. Two trains collided in Bekasi, leaving four dead and 38 passengers rushed to nearby hospitals.

item.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
item.description: A bomb blast on a Colombian highway killed 21 people and injured 56, blamed on cocaine-trafficking rebels. The attack was the country's worst on civilians in decades, ahead of elections.

item.title: Bahrain strips dozens of citizenship over Iran support
item.description: Bahrain's Ministry stripped dozens of people of their citizenship, citing support for hostile Iranian acts, including colluding with foreign entities. The list included dependents, but exact numbers weren't specified.

item.title: At U.N., U.S. Calls for Coalition of Maritime Freedom On Hormuz
item.description: The US called for a coalition to ensure maritime freedom in the Strait of Hormuz at the UN. The initiative aims to secure shipping lanes in the critical waterway. At U. N., U. S.

item.title: Gunmen kidnap 23 children from Nigerian orphanage
item.description: Gunmen kidnapped 23 children from a Nigerian orphanage. Mass kidnappings are a common tactic used by gangs and armed groups to make quick money in Nigeria. Mass kidnappings are a common way for gangs and armed groups to make quick money in Nigeria.

item.title: Hezbollah Vows to Keep Its Weapons as Lebanon Says Israeli Strikes Killed 14
item.description: Hezbollah leader Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah stated that they will keep their weapons. This comes as Israel and Hezbollah have been trading daily attacks despite a cease-fire, with 14 reported killed in recent Israeli strikes.
